title: Wall Street closes mixed with Nasdaq up driven by semiconductors and Dow Jones down
summary: U.S. indices showed mixed results in a session marked by corporate earnings season and geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, with the technology sector leading gains.

title: A U.S. judge temporarily suspends the merger of Warner Bros and Paramount
summary: A U.S. court has ordered a temporary halt to the acquisition of Warner Bros by Paramount-Skydance until August 3.

title: Houthis announce a naval blockade against Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea
summary: The Iran-backed militia declared a maritime ban against the Saudi kingdom, raising regional tensions and generating fears about energy supply.

title: The Ibex 35 closes practically flat amid uncertainty over the Middle East
summary: The Spanish benchmark remained virtually unchanged, affected by the escalation of tensions in the Middle East and awaiting corporate results.

title: Oil surpasses $90 and gasoline in the U.S. returns to $4 per gallon due to the Iran conflict
summary: Crude oil and fuel prices soared in response to Iranian attacks on tankers and the intensification of the war in the Middle East.

title: Bitcoin rises to near $64,000 amid mixed outlook
summary: The leading cryptocurrency stood at $63,859, in a context of divided opinions about its evolution for the remainder of 2026.

title: Morgan Stanley leverages the AI boom to close million-dollar debt deals
summary: The investment bank is capitalizing on the demand for AI infrastructure by structuring debt financing for technology companies.

title: European bond yields rise on higher oil prices and expectations of increased public spending
summary: The yield on the German 10-year bond and British gilts increased due to the pressure from rising crude prices and the forecast of an increase in fiscal spending in the United Kingdom.
